response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned generalist with nearly two decades of experience, but his background is overwhelmingly web and full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, Ember) and misses the role's hard requirements. He lists no Japanese proficiency, which is a non-negotiable native-level requirement; he shows no Linux kernel modification experience, no LAN-switch or embedded networking work, and no security-feature experience (MACsec, 802.1X, encryption, authentication). His C/C++ exposure is brief and dated (a ~7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 and a thesis project), far short of the strong C-for-UNIX proficiency the role demands.
strengths: 18+ years of broad software experience across web, mobile, desktop, and systems, with senior/staff-level roles
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work (DLang microservices handling millions of requests/second) and solid Linux familiarity
Strong engineering culture: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling authorship, and knowledge-sharing through workshops
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency, a required native-level qualification for this role
No embedded networking, LAN-switch, Linux kernel, or security-technology (MACsec, 802.1X, encryption) experience
C programming experience is minimal and dated; recent work is entirely web/full-stack in TypeScript and Node.js

key_factors.factor: Broad senior engineering experience with some C/C++ and Linux background
